rules.statement: Always match database field names exactly between application code and Supabase tables to avoid silent failures
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Silent data loss or insertion failures where application code references non-existent columns, causing writes to fail without clear error messages

rules.statement: Always save the .env file after inputting variables; auto-save may not be enabled
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Application reads stale or empty environment variables, causing API calls to fail with authentication errors or undefined behavior

rules.statement: Test API integrations early and in isolation before building UI on top of them
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: UI is built on assumptions about API response format that turn out to be incorrect, requiring costly rework of both API handling and UI layers simultaneously

rules.statement: Be specific with the agent about data formats (JSON structure, field names) or it will improvise
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Agent generates mismatched data structures that do not align with database schemas or API contracts, leading to runtime errors and integration failures

rules.statement: When implementing multiple phases, require the agent to mark phases done and get approval before proceeding
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Agent races ahead through multiple phases, compounding errors from earlier phases into later ones, making debugging exponentially harder

rules.statement: Disable RLS in Supabase during development for simplicity, but re-enable before production
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: If left disabled in production, any authenticated user can read or modify any row in the database, exposing all user data

rules.statement: Never let a system send emails on your behalf; always have a human in the loop for outbound communications
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Automated system sends incorrect, offensive, or misleading emails to customers or partners, causing reputational damage and potential legal liability

rules.statement: Set appropriate data retention periods; push back on 'keep everything forever' unless legally required
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Unbounded data storage increases costs, expands attack surface for data breaches, and may violate privacy regulations like GDPR

rules.statement: Build and test all skills and dependencies before packaging for distribution
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Distributed package contains broken skills or missing dependencies, causing failures for end users and eroding trust in the product

rules.statement: Test at two levels: functionality-level in the IDE, and front-end testing after distribution
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Features that work in the IDE break in the distributed environment due to path differences, missing configs, or environment-specific issues

rules.statement: Bake in basic PII/API key sanitization into the memory pipeline automatically before storage
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Sensitive data such as API keys, passwords, or personal information is stored in plaintext in memory systems, creating a security breach vector

heuristics.condition: IF the AI creates the database table via SQL
heuristics.action: THEN fields match; if you create manually, expect mismatches
heuristics.reason: AI-generated SQL aligns with its own code generation patterns, while manual table creation introduces human naming conventions that may diverge from what the AI expects

heuristics.condition: IF the API returns a CORS error from the browser
heuristics.action: THEN create a server-side API route instead
heuristics.reason: Browser-based API calls are blocked by CORS policies on most third-party APIs; routing through a server-side endpoint bypasses this restriction

heuristics.condition: IF Perplexity API returns a 'thinking' tag
heuristics.action: THEN the parser must strip it before processing
heuristics.reason: The thinking tag is internal chain-of-thought content not meant for end users; including it corrupts the parsed output and confuses downstream consumers

heuristics.condition: IF database and API setup is done
heuristics.action: THEN UI implementation is usually easy for the AI
heuristics.reason: Once data contracts are established and APIs are verified, the AI can generate UI components confidently because the input/output shapes are known

heuristics.condition: IF implementing many things at once
heuristics.action: THEN issues compound; implement phases individually with testing
heuristics.reason: Multiple simultaneous changes create combinatorial failure modes that are extremely difficult to debug; sequential implementation with testing gates isolates failures

heuristics.condition: IF a newer memory contradicts an older one
heuristics.action: THEN temporal decay should weight the newer memory higher
heuristics.reason: More recent information typically reflects the current state of the system, user preferences, or corrected understanding; older memories may be outdated or superseded

heuristics.condition: IF a feature is large (multiple tables, APIs, UI)
heuristics.action: THEN use plan mode first to increase chances of one-shotting it
heuristics.reason: Planning upfront identifies dependencies, sequencing, and potential issues before code generation begins, reducing the number of iterations needed

frameworks.name: SaaS MVP Build Pipeline
steps: Data ingestion via N8N workflows
Build messaging UI for user interaction
Integrate external APIs (Perplexity, etc.)
Parse and display API responses in the UI
Generate reports from processed data
UI polish and user experience refinement

frameworks.name: Phased Development with Testing Gates
steps: Define numbered phases in a to-do file
Agent completes all steps within a single phase
Agent marks the phase as done
Human reviews and approves the completed phase
Agent proceeds to next phase only after approval
Repeat until all phases are complete

frameworks.name: API Integration Testing Pattern
steps: Add a temporary test button to the UI
Wire the button to trigger the target API call
Check the browser console and network tab for response data
Debug any issues with the API call or response parsing
Remove the test button once integration is verified
Proceed with building the production UI on top of the verified API

frameworks.name: Scrappy Stack Progression
steps: Start with N8N + Google Sheets for rapid prototyping
Graduate to Lovable + N8N + Supabase for structured MVP
Evolve to Cursor/IDE-based MVP for full production readiness

frameworks.name: Hybrid Memory System
steps: Implement 70% vector search via Pine Cone for semantic similarity
Implement 30% keyword search via BM25 for exact matches
Apply temporal decay to weight newer memories higher
Run deduplication to remove redundant or overlapping memories
Apply secret scrubbing to sanitize PII and API keys before storage
Combine results from both search methods for retrieval

frameworks.name: Memory Loop
steps: User talks to the AI agent
Stop hook fires at the end of the conversation
Scrub secrets and PII from the conversation content
Extract facts via Mem Zero + GPT-4.1 nano
Deduplicate extracted facts against existing memory store
Store deduplicated facts in the memory backend
At next session start, query memory store for relevant context
Inject top matching memories into the system prompt

tech_patterns.name: Production Stack
tech_patterns.description: Full production technology stack for AI-powered SaaS applications combining Next.js for the frontend, Supabase with Row Level Security for the database, N8N for email processing workflows, Perplexity API for deep research capabilities, and Claude Code for development acceleration

tech_patterns.name: N8N Workflow Pattern
tech_patterns.description: Automated email processing pipeline that triggers on new Gmail messages, classifies content using AI, extracts brand and sender data, cleans the extracted data, and writes structured results to Supabase tables

tech_patterns.name: Perplexity API Config
tech_patterns.description: Recommended Perplexity API configuration for deep research use cases using the sonar-deep-research model with web search mode enabled and structured JSON schema response format for reliable parsing

tech_patterns.name: Hooks Architecture
tech_patterns.description: Event-driven hook system for AI agent lifecycle management: stop hooks capture memory at conversation end, pre-tool hooks enforce guardrails before tool execution, and post-tool hooks validate outputs after tool execution

tech_patterns.name: Hybrid Search Stack
tech_patterns.description: Dual-mode search system combining 70% vector similarity search via Pine Cone for semantic matching with 30% keyword-based BM25 search for exact term matching, enhanced with temporal decay to prioritize recent information

tech_patterns.name: Plugin Distribution System
tech_patterns.description: Claude Co-work plugin distribution model where users customize via tabs for skills, plugins, and connectors, with support for uploading zip archives or linking GitHub repositories for package delivery

tech_patterns.name: SeaTable
tech_patterns.description: Open source alternative to Airtable used as a lightweight CRM solution during the scrappy prototyping phase before graduating to a full database like Supabase

tech_patterns.name: Headless Browser Control
tech_patterns.description: Using Claude Code to control a headless browser for automation steps that lack dedicated APIs, enabling web scraping, form filling, and UI interaction programmatically

tech_patterns.name: Sub Agents
tech_patterns.description: Pattern where Claude spawns separate AI instances to handle parallel subtasks, each operating with its own independent context window to maximize throughput and reduce wall-clock time for complex operations

metrics.name: Typical feature build time with Claude Code
